trainees.name: Michael Wilson
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, snap/back bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, arm drags, hip tosses, drop-toe-hold (giver), DDT (give & take), enzuigiri, big boots, punches/strikes, drop kicks, front/back/superman rolls, fireman's carry (teaches it), suplex (demo form), body slam (give), kitchen sink (take), sentons, time warp (took for the first time), four-corner selling, promos, five-minute practice matches (heel).

**Recent flags:** None on his own work. July 7, June 30, and June 23 were all clean; across recent sessions he's more often the one teaching or demonstrating than being corrected.

Michael is the de facto senior coach of the gym. 114 sessions in, he draws essentially zero correction on his own work and is regularly used by the trainers to demonstrate and teach — leading the group through the fireman's carry, coaching newer trainees through spot one, and anchoring clean heel practice matches. He calls himself a 'jack of all trades, master of none,' and functions as the program's model for self-driven ringwork.

trainees.name: Hailey Stokely
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, selling, forearms/strikes, DDT (give & take), drop kicks, suplex (take), body slam (give/take, used as demo model), second-rope hurricanrana, second-rope frog splash, super kick, spot one (coaches it), leapfrog (jumper), four-corner selling, promos, five-minute practice matches (heel).

**Recent flags:** None recently on her own work. July 7, June 30, and June 16 were clean; her only recent self-inflicted clip was a slightly off hand placement on her first frog splash back on May 12.

Hailey is a veteran trainee operating at near-trainer level. She draws almost no correction and increasingly does the correcting — leading chain-wrestling instruction, refining newer trainees' form, and catching timing and safety issues before the trainers do. She leads and calls her practice matches, works a polished heel, and is currently locking in the hurricanrana and a satisfaction ahead of the next TAW show. She sets the standard in the room.

trainees.name: Jacob Justice
trainees.notes: Marked inactive May 12, 2026 after six consecutive absences from April 7 onward. Not expected to return to training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** selling, rope-throw drills, bumps.

**Recent flags:** Flagged as timid and slow on rope-throw drills (April 7) and as an injury risk for landing elbow-first on bumps. No sessions since to work on either.

Jacob's selling improved visibly through early spring, but confidence and pacing were his biggest gaps and he hasn't attended a session since April 7 — six-plus consecutive absences. Marked inactive May 12; not expected back. History preserved.

trainees.name: Tyler Skelton
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups & chain wrestling, front/superman rolls, headlock takeovers, drop kicks, suplex (take & give), body slam (give), drop-toe-hold (giver), face bumps (take), kick + back forearm, German suplex, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** June 30 — sat out the practice matches after hurting his knee at work (not a training injury). June 12 — struggled to get enough jump height on drop kicks, a size/explosiveness edge to keep working.

Tyler is a big-frame trainee still learning how his height and size shape his ringwork — most of his coaching is about explosiveness (drop-kick height) and playing his size in matches. He's solid and believable when he's on: a standout technical five-minute match against Michael in May, clean lockups, and a German suplex that came together cleanly. Recently limited by a work knee injury.

trainees.name: Jay Taylor
trainees.notes: Removed from program April 14, 2026. Blackballed from local wrestling scene due to unsafe work, lack of attendance, refusal to accept criticism.

trainees.name: DC
trainees.notes: Removed from the training program May 15, 2026 by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote following the May 12 promo incident (personal insults toward Hailey). Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel determined Wrestler's Court was the most impartial way to handle the decision. DC pled his case in front of the trainees; each trainee voted in the locker room with reasoning. DC's defense involved disturbing allegations — locker-room vote notes kept separately at Joey's call, not in this record. Prior conduct flag: May 12 promo, called Hailey a bitch, crossed other personal lines.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups, headlock, two-person rope-crossing drill, spot one, promos.

**Recent flags:** Conduct was the decisive issue — the May 12 promo against Hailey crossed hard personal lines (tried to make her go first 'because she's a woman' despite her being the heel, then called her a bitch). Technically, fundamentals still needed polish: stiff lockups, head dropping in the headlock, drifting on the rope-crossing drill, and timing/coordination off on his first spot-one attempt.

DC was removed from the program by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote on May 15, 2026. He met with Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel about the May 12 promo incident, then pled his case to the trainees before the vote. His technique was still developing, but the removal was about conduct, not ringwork — personal insults were made clear as a hard line.

trainees.name: Dalton
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockups, strikes/punches, big boots, DDT (give), drop kicks, enzuigiri, headlock takeovers, front/back/superman rolls, snap bumps, flip bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, hip tosses, arm drags, dropdowns, suplex (take), face bumps, double downs, four-corner selling, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 7 — rough spot-one night: bad finger-lock form in the headlock, not switching his feet, forgetting who takes the shoulder tackle, rushing the restart, running crooked; also flagged for doing the same moves all around the ring on four corners, and his promo fizzled after ~45 seconds. June 30 — reminded to keep his head up in the headlock; in his match, flagged for skipping a called move (Hailey) and for not selling enough (Cody). June 23 — slow on dropdowns, hip-toss height/selling/crookedness, plus in-match no-selling and not moving enough.

Dalton is consistently the most coaching-intensive trainee in the program — he absorbs more correction than anyone, but he absorbs it within the session and keeps improving. His recurring edges are selling, staying square (not running crooked), and remembering spot placements under speed. When it clicks he strings together genuinely clean work (his enzuigiri really came together in June), and he's earned the occasional unprompted praise. Steady grinder who needs reps but keeps taking them.

trainees.name: Jack
trainees.notes: Age 14. Non-bumping restriction lifted May 5, 2026 — parent always present at training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** chops, bumps & selling, spot one, hip toss, DDT (take & give), strikes, drop kicks, enzuigiri (+ step-up), lockups, headlock takeovers (give), headlock-to-wrist-control, kitchen sink (take), front/back/superman rolls, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat-drill selling, suplex (take), body slam (take), flip bumps, drop-toe-hold (give & take), chain wrestling, promos.

**Recent flags:** Mostly medical, not technical — after a hospital visit he was on light work only June 23 (limited, largely off the notes) and June 30 (shortened chain-wrestling session, sat out matches). Back to fuller participation July 7, where the only notes were a suggestion to slap the mat on his bump for more noise and to jump a little more into the hip toss.

Jack is 14 and on a clear fast track. His signature pattern is brief coaching followed by clean execution — first-ever DDT, back rolls, headlock takeovers, and a kitchen sink all landed on minimal instruction. He's a standout on the mic (asked to cut a second promo on the fly July 7, just as strong) and rides confidence well once a psychological block is cleared, as with the crash-pad suplex breakthrough. Recently limited by a hospital stay but returning to full participation. Parent always present.

trainees.name: Nick
trainees.notes: Jack's dad. Officially joined the program April 14, 2026. Left the program June 2, 2026 after disclosing pre-existing back and nerve problems — decided he didn't want wrestling to exacerbate them. History preserved.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** snap bumps, lockups (incl. lockup-to-corner push), headlock, face bumps, drop-toe-hold (give & take), body slam (give & take), five-minute practice match (babyface).

**Recent flags:** May 26 — ghost bumped (bumped too early) on his first snap bump, landed wrong, and hurt his back, sitting out the rest of the night. That early-bump habit had now caused an injury.

Nick — Jack's dad — was one of the easiest trainees in the gym to coach, usually absorbing corrections on the first rep, and had grown from rep work into holding his own in a full five-minute practice match (a clean babyface comeback win over heel Hailey). He left the program June 2, 2026 after disclosing pre-existing back and nerve problems, deciding he didn't want wrestling to worsen them; the May 26 back injury likely factored in. History preserved.

trainees.name: Brandon
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Has been training for a while and has worked a few matches at TAW.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** front/back rolls, flip bumps, four-corner selling, chain wrestling, dropdowns, hip tosses, arm drags, lockups, DDT (give), drop kicks, enzuigiri, superman rolls, headlock takeovers (give), kitchen sink (take), big boots, double downs, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat drill, spot one, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 10 — told to diversify his moves on the four-corner drill (Jimmy then demonstrated how varied the move set can be). June 23 — flagged for slowness on dropdowns (more an opening-pairing issue; much better once Cody ran the ropes for him) and told to watch his legs/leg placement in his match. June 2 — his promo started well but fell flat when he ran out of material.

Brandon joined from Cody McCulley's roster with prior TAW match experience, and it shows in how cleanly and quickly he absorbs new material. Eleven sessions in, most of his work draws no notes; his growth areas are broadening his in-ring vocabulary (four-corner variety) and promo stamina. Reliable, low-maintenance, and already comfortable working full practice matches on both sides.

trainees.name: Jeff
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Relatively new to training.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** five and fives, front rolls, flip bumps, hip tosses, four-corner selling, chain wrestling, arm drags (take), DDT (give), Irish whips, drop kicks, headlock takeovers (give), big boots, punches, double downs, back rolls, snap bumps, shoulder tackles, back elbows, heat drill, drop-toe-hold (give), lockups, suplex (form work), spot one, promos, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 10 — Cody wants a proper flip bump, not the 'fat boy flip bump' form Jeff originally learned; he has a good base and is refining it. July 7 — shared one spot-one flag with Dalton (forgetting who takes the shoulder tackle), otherwise clean. June 30 — reminded about headlock head placement on chain wrestling, then did great.

Jeff is relatively new and his current growth edge is bumping mechanics — flip bumps and getting enough jump height (which has held back his suplex). But the trajectory is clearly up: he took the best bump guest trainer Cade has seen from him in May, picks up corrections fast on the next rep, and has already won a five-minute practice match. Solid, coachable, and steadily tightening his fundamentals.

trainees.name: Damian
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 2, 2026. Name was previously spelled 'Damien' in earlier notes; corrected to 'Damian' June 16, 2026.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** bumps & selling, spot one, four-corner selling, promos, chain wrestling, lockup-to-headlock, strikes, DDT (give & take), drop kicks, enzuigiri, double downs, big boots, punches, back rolls, lockups, five-minute practice matches.

**Recent flags:** July 7 — four-corner selling was a little robotic (Kytel; Cade demonstrated), improved when Dalton worked him. June 30 — flagged for not selling enough in his match (kicked out too strong without selling the offense). June 23 — flagged with his opponent for strike work, and specifically for stomping the heel when he was supposed to be working face (staying in character).

Damian is a promising new trainee six sessions in, with a strong all-around night on July 7 (very well-executed spot one, a promo timed almost to the minute). He named bumps as his strength and Jimmy has used him to teach that you sell even in training. His current lessons are the finer points of selling and staying in character during matches — normal edges this early. Coming along quickly.

trainees.name: Jacob Renzo
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 2, 2026. Distinct from former trainee Jacob Justice (inactive). Removed from the program June 30, 2026 for booking himself in a match at a promotion without express consent from Cody or Jimmy, despite only having two training sessions under his belt.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockup → headlock → shoulder tackle, selling, strikes, enzuigiri, double downs, big boots, punches, back rolls, lockups, promos.

**Recent flags:** June 16 — Jimmy noticed something off on his enzuigiri; Kaleb spotted he was planting his foot, and after the correction his reps were good (just needs a bit more leg height). His actual work drew little criticism — the issue that ended his run was conduct, not ringwork.

Jacob Renzo showed well in his three sessions — great selling with no notes, clean strikes, and quick corrections. He was removed from the program June 30, 2026 after booking himself in a match at an outside promotion without permission from Cody or Jimmy, with only two training sessions in. Jimmy used the removal as a teaching point (Hailey cited the Mass Transit vs. New Jack ECW incident as a cautionary example). Distinct from former trainee Jacob Justice.

trainees.name: Jaden
trainees.notes: New trainee, joined June 16, 2026. Was previously in the program in 2025 before stepping away from wrestling entirely in October 2025; because of the long gap she is counted as a brand-new trainee in the system, with this prior experience noted here.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** lockup → headlock → shoulder tackle, selling, strikes, DDT (take & give), drop kicks, enzuigiri.

**Recent flags:** June 16 (debut) — needs to make more sound while selling; strikes needed work on form and being more animated; struggled to get enough height/rotation on drop kicks (Jimmy had her drill the jump-and-turn separately before adding the kick back in, which worked).

Jaden is returning to the program after stepping away from wrestling in late 2025, and is counted as a brand-new trainee despite that prior experience. Her one logged session was a solid debut — clean on the lockup drill, and she worked through her drop-kick and DDT issues within the night. Early days; skills and flags will fill in as she logs more sessions.

trainees.name: Jbird
trainees.notes: New trainee, first session June 30, 2026. 'Jbird' is the only name on record so far — full/real name TBD.
trainees.current_summary:
**Skills worked:** chain wrestling.

**Recent flags:** June 30 (debut) — coached through chain wrestling on pacing, foot placement, following Kytel's lead, and general form/placement; Brent also worked with him one-on-one. All normal first-timer coaching.

Jbird is brand new — a promising debut on June 30 paired with guest trainer Kytel, called a good first attempt overall, with the expected first-timer coaching load. Did not take part in the practice matches. Full/real name still TBD.
